violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

Establishment is using a spray hose to fill sanitizer basin of 3 bay sink. Faucet head required. Inadequate pressure could result in improper equipment operation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2025
Wall cap near storage area is in poor repair and held by tape. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -gaskets for 3 door reach-in -shelving holster for 3 door reach-in Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

3 door reach-in in kitchen is not maintaining temperature to hold TCS foods. Unit was out of service to hold foods that require refrigeration for food safety from prior inspection. Equipment to be repaired/ adjusted/ replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the cold holding unit condenser has an accumulation of dust and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

Establishment is using a spray hose to fill sanitizer basin of 3 bay sink. Faucet head required. Inadequate pressure could result in improper equipment operation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2025
Wall cap near storage area is in poor repair and held by tape. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

Buffalo chicken, string cheese, breakfast sandwiches, and other foods were observed held at 45-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Foods recently prepared and/or recently delivered were placed into other refrigeration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -gaskets for 3 door reach-in -shelving holster for 3 door reach-in Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025

3 door reach-in was observed at ranging ambient temperatures of 43-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service to hold foods that require refrigeration for food safety. Equipment to be repaired/ adjusted/ replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -interior of 3 door reach-in -cold holding unit condenser (dust) -wall near trash barrel New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

Establishment is using a spray hose to fill sanitizer basin of 3 bay sink. Faucet head required. Inadequate pressure could result in improper equipment operation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2025
Wall cap near storage area is in poor repair and held by tape. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/09/2025
Drain stoppers for 3 bay sink are in poor repair. Drain stoppers must be in good repair to effectively wash, rinse and sanitize equipment and utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025

Establishment is using a spray hose to fill sanitizer basin of 3 bay sink. Faucet head required. Inadequate pressure could result in improper equipment operation. New Violation. Correct By: 09/28/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Once certificate is obtained, PIC to register Rhode Island Food Protection Manager license. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Personal belongings were observed in the warewashing area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2024
Establishment has a Serv-Safe certification not registered with Rhode Island Department of Health. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

The ventilation system and AC system at the kitchen and preparation area is in poor repair. Heating, ventilating, and air conditioning systems shall be designed and installed so that make-up air intake and exhaust. vents do not cause contamination of FOOD, FOOD-CONTACT SURFACES, EQUIPMENT, or UTENSILS. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by mice droppings at the kitchen floor and dry storage. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2023

The floor under racks and storage room area has an accumulation of some mice droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/18/2023
